token_note_1: Note: Please enter the token and the password (yes, you read that right, both fields are password inputs).
token_note_2: The first field is the UFI-TOOLS login token (default is admin).
token_note_3: The second field is the ZXE backend administrator password (can be found on the device label).
token_note_4: If a password error occurs, please try a different login method.
token_placeholder_token: Token (check in the UFI-TOOLS backend application)
band_tip: 🚀 This setting does not require reboot. After changes, just switch the network mode 🚀
cell_tip: 🚀 Warning: Locking wrong cell may cause no or unstable signal 🚀
cell_tip1: Tip: After pausing refresh, you can lock multiple base stations one by one.
plugin_modal_tip: The entered content supports scripts, styles, and meta elements. The total size of all plugins must not exceed 1145KB. Click the plugin name to edit, enable or disable. Drag on the left to sort, delete plugins on the right.
client_mgmt_fetch_error: Failed to fetch data. Please check network connection.
at_warning_1: ⚠️ Warning: Custom AT commands are advanced operations. Please ensure the input is accurate. You are responsible for any consequences.
at_warning_2: ⚠️ Note: AT command (same as network ADB) supported versions: F50:B09\B24 U30:B17\B19 M3:B07. If not working, please provide your firmware version to author.
advance_warning: ⚠️ Warning: This feature is intended for advanced users who are familiar with the device. General users should not attempt to use it casually, as it may cause device malfunction, data loss, or irreversible issues.
advance_feature_list: ⚠️ Note: After enabling advanced features, do not disable the file sharing switch, otherwise advanced features will stop working!! Advanced Features: Optimized Samba directories (including external storage), TTYD terminal (port 1146, built-in RootShell), blocking sensitive ports from IPv6 access, support for scheduled/boot scripts. If disabled with errors, please run sh /sdcard/unlock_samba.sh and reboot. (Thanks @AiCherub)
smtp_hint: Recipient email can be the same as the sender
curl_hint: Note: The command must be input in one go. Do not press Enter or use \ to continue the line. Pay attention to spaces between parameters.
update_notice_1: Software updates require enabling Advanced Features (for fast installation) or Network ADB debugging (restart the device, or disable and re-enable USB debugging). If the one-click update function does not work properly, you can click to download the installation package and install it manually.
update_notice_2: Due to Android's package management system, do not power off during update. Make sure the device enters the system properly after update before restarting.
pay_tip_1: Development is not easy, feel free to tip if you'd like~
pay_tip_2: Long-press the QR code to save it as an image
status_modal_tip: Note: Items unsupported by the device or with empty values will not be displayed even if selected. If you encounter missing fields, try resetting or clearing your browser data.
adb_not_init: ADB is not initialized. Please wait for initialization to complete; or enable Advanced features directly for ultra-fast installation!
use_adb_to_disabled_update: Used ADB to disable update. For stronger disable, enable advanced mode
cellular_speed_test_notice: Speed test is relayed via the intranet proxy, for reference only. Due to proxy API limits, each download lasts only 30 seconds.
plugin_save_fail_network: Plugin save failed, please check network
not_login_not_save_plugin: Not logged in, plugin will not be saved
no_dingtalk_url: Please enter the DingTalk Webhook URL
dingtalk_hint: Configure the DingTalk group bot webhook URL, signing supported
toast_connect_failed: Failed to connect to backend, check network
error_loading_plugins: Error loading plugins. Please check your network
plugin_store_tip: The plugin store offers more plugin features. You can download and install them here.
plugin_store_tip1: Please note that installing plugins may affect device performance and stability. Choose carefully.
plugin_store_tip2: Some plugins require advanced features to be enabled before use.
need_advance_func: Advanced features need to be enabled
not_support_firmware: Your system version is not supported. Some features may not work properly. Please update your Pocket WiFi system to a compatible version!
useTerms:
Software Name: UFI-TOOLS
License: MIT License
Applicable to: All users who download, install, run, modify, or distribute this tool (hereinafter referred to as 'Users').

1. Nature of the Software

UFI-TOOLS is an open-source tool provided “as is”, designed to provide functional management for ZXE and other portable WiFi devices (F50, U30 Air, V50, U20 (F50 firmware version), and M3 (firmware version ≤ B07)).
This project is developed and maintained by an individual, is not affiliated with, and has not obtained authorization or support from any manufacturer.

2. Risk Notice

Using this tool may involve the following potential risks (including but not limited to):
Upgrading OTA, installing plugins, or flashing firmware may cause the device to malfunction, behave abnormally, or become permanently damaged.
Unofficial firmware or third-party plugins may contain security vulnerabilities, compatibility issues, or content that violates applicable laws or regulations.
During operation, the device may experience data loss, configuration loss, or network disconnection.
Network transmission may be interrupted or fail due to force majeure, including but not limited to network outages, server failures, or software defects.
Users should fully understand these risks and back up their data before performing any operations.

3. Data Collection & Privacy

To improve the software and troubleshoot issues, this tool may collect the following information:
Device IP address (for debugging and network connectivity)
Device model, software version, and firmware version (for compatibility analysis)
Local log records (not uploaded to any remote server), including device running status, error messages, executed commands, etc.

Notes:
The collected data is used solely for technical analysis and troubleshooting, and is not used for commercial purposes.
This project does not guarantee absolute security of data transmission or storage. Users bear all risks arising therefrom.
If the user does not wish to share such information, they should disconnect from the network or avoid using features that require internet access.

4. Limitation of Liability

This tool is provided 'as is', without any express or implied warranties, including but not limited to merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, defect-free operation, or non-infringement.
The developer and contributors shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from the use of this tool, including but not limited to device damage, data loss, service interruption, business interruption, or financial losses.
This project does not provide any form of commercial support and does not commit to resolving all issues users may encounter.

5. User Responsibility

Users must determine for themselves whether this tool is suitable for their needs, and fully understand the operating methods and potential risks before use.
Users must comply with the laws and regulations of their jurisdiction when using this tool, and must not use it for any illegal purposes.
Users are solely responsible for all operations they perform and the resulting consequences.

6. Changes to the Terms

This disclaimer may be updated at any time without prior notice. The updated version will take effect immediately upon release.
Final Reminder: If you do not agree with the above terms, please do not download, install, or use this tool.
please_accept_terms: Please agree to the ‘User Agreement and Disclaimer’
browser_version_low: The core version is too low, some features may not work properly. Please update your browser.
browser_version_very_low: The core version is too low, some features may not work properly. Please update your browser.
tg_sms_help:
UFITOOLS TG bot SMS forwarding request method:
Example code (CURL method):
curl -s -X POST https://api.telegram.org/bot/sendMessage -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"chat_id":your chat id,"text":"{{sms-body}} {{sms-time}} {{sms-from}}","parse_mode":"HTML"}'
Create a new Bot
Find @BotFather in Telegram
This is Telegram's official bot management account
Open search, enter @BotFather, click its avatar
Chat with BotFather, first enter: /start
Then enter: /newbot

Follow the prompts to enter:
Bot display name (any, e.g., MyTestBot)
Bot username (must end with bot, e.g., mytest_robot_bot)

BotFather will return information, the most important is:
Use this token to access the HTTP API:
123456789:ABCdefGhIJKlmNoPQRstuVWXyz
This long string is the Bot Token (required to send messages)

Get Chat ID (recipient)
Send any message with the Bot you created
Then access: https://api.telegram.org/bot/getUpdates

In the returned JSON, find: "chat": {
"id": 987654321,
"first_name": "...",
...
}
id is your chat_id
wechat_sms_help:
WeCom Webhook SMS forwarding tutorial:

Get the group bot Key: Add a bot in your WeCom group and copy the generated Webhook URL, like https://qyapi.weixin.qq.com/cgi-bin/webhook/send?key=yourkey

Send SMS using curl, one line, no line breaks, no \:
curl -X POST "https://qyapi.weixin.qq.com/cgi-bin/webhook/send?key=" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"msgtype": "text", "text": {"content": "【Number】{{sms-from}}\n【SMS content】{{sms-body}}\n【Time】{{sms-time}}"}}'

Parameter explanation: {{sms-from}} sender number, {{sms-body}} message content, {{sms-time}} receive time. Replace variables after receiving SMS to send to WeCom group.
pushplus_sms_help:
PushPlus SMS forwarding tutorial:

Get PushPlus Token: Login to https://www.pushplus.plus/, register an account, create a push bot, and copy the generated Token.

Send SMS using curl, one line, no line breaks, no \:
curl -s -X POST https://www.pushplus.plus/send/ -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" -d "token=&title=New message!!&content=【SMS content】%0A{{sms-body}}%0A%0A**【Time】%0A{{sms-time}}%0A%0A【Number】**%0A{{sms-from}}&template=markdown"

Parameter explanation: {{sms-from}} sender number, {{sms-body}} message content, {{sms-time}} receive time. Replace variables after receiving SMS to send to PushPlus.
bark_sms_help:
Bark SMS forwarding tutorial:

Get Bark Token: Download the Bark app from the App Store, find your token on the server page, and copy it.

Reference: https://github.com/finb/bark
Parameter description: {{sms-from}} sender number, {{sms-body}} SMS content, {{sms-time}} receive time. After receiving an SMS, replace the variables and send it to Bark.
login_help_text:
To ensure device security, logging into the backend requires entering both a Passcode and an Admin Password.
1.	Passcode
•	Default is "admin". For security, please change it to a strong password.
•	You can cast screen to open the UFI-TOOLS_WEB app and change it there.
•	Click "Stop Service" to display the password change field.

2.	Admin Password
•	This is the device’s backend management password.
•	The default value is printed on the back of the device.

3.	Login Methods
•	You can choose Login Method 1 or Login Method 2 from the dropdown.
•	If one method shows "password error", try the other.
•	Method 1 has no session timeout, better performance, medium compatibility.
•	Method 2 offers better compatibility, but sessions last 5 minutes (auto re-login, data may freeze 0.5s).

4.	Common Issues
•	If you see "Password Error":
•	Check if input is correct (case-sensitive).
•	Try switching login methods.
•	If password is forgotten, check the factory info on device back, or contact admin.
please_input_full_profile: Please fill in Profile Name & APN Name & Authentication Type & PDP Type
apn_title_info: The Access Point Name (APN) is the name your device uses to connect to your carrier’s network. This information is automatically matched based on your SIM card. If you cannot access the internet, try adjusting these parameters. For details, please consult your local carrier.
toast_token_not_eqal: The two entries do not match
usb_status_notice: Only displays devices connected through the Type-C port. Data is for reference only.
weak_token_detected: A weak password was detected. You must change it to keep your device secure.
token_change_notice: Password must be at least 8 characters and include letters and numbers.
toast_token_invalid: Password must include letters and numbers!
toast_token_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters!
three_days_later_info: To ensure the user updates the passcode, this reminder will appear again in three days.
remind_your_token_text: Please take a screenshot or securely keep your password to prevent forgetting it.
force_update_desc: Note: This is a forced prompt. If you’ve updated, ignore it. It will auto-disable in a few hours.
upload_success_but_cannot_detect_file_na: Upload successful, but cannot detect file name
need_advanced_func_use_dev_menu: Some features require enabling advanced functions in the developer menu
action_forward_dev_info_notice: Device info forwarding depends on SMS forwarding
action_forward_dev_info_notice_fail: SMS forwarding is not enabled. Please enable and configure it on the "SMS Forwarding" page first.
data_manage_tips: Tip: Please set the reminder threshold correctly.
data_manage_tips1: When data usage reaches the set threshold percentage, the device will flash a red light to remind you that the data is running low.
